description: At 00:00:00, the game is in progress. No yellow cards visible yet. Referee is on the pitch.
Referee in a red shirt is visible standing with team captains (Messi and Mbappe) in cells 40-47. This confirms the presence of the referee for the search task.
Referees (Szymon Marciniak and others) are shown during the match official introduction from 36.7s to 38.5s. This helps identify the officials who would be issuing any yellow cards.
At 86:31 (and continuing to 86:32), the referee shows a yellow card to a French player (Marcus Thuram). This is a definitive instance of a yellow card being given.

At 103.0s, the referee is visible in the last two frames of the collage. He is wearing a red shirt and has his hand raised, but no yellow card is being shown. This helps the search task by identifying a referee instance, though it doesn't directly answer the yellow card count yet.
A referee in a red shirt is visible in several frames (around 03:17-03:18 on the game clock). He is not showing a yellow card in these frames. This helps identify the referee and the match context.
At 00:01:46, the referee shows a yellow card to a player (Argentina #23, Emiliano Martínez). This is the first yellow card observed.

At 00:01:57, the referee shows a yellow card to a player (France #11, Ousmane Dembélé - wait, no, looking closer, it's a player on the bench or recently subbed? No, it's Leandro Paredes #5 for Argentina). Let me re-examine. Actually, at 01:57, the referee shows a yellow card to Leandro Paredes.
The referee in a yellow shirt is visible near a tackle in cells 14-18 (approx 122.5s - 124s). This is a potential moment for a yellow card.

At 26:58 (game time), the referee (Szymon Marciniak) is visible in the center of the frame, signaling with his hands. No yellow card is shown in this specific sequence. This helps the search task by identifying a visual instance of the referee.
A referee in a red shirt is visible in cell 37 (around 295.2s) during the goal celebration. No yellow card is shown in this specific timeframe.
The referee is visible in the wide shots of the pitch (e.g., cell 2, 10) wearing a bright yellow/green kit. This helps identify the referee's appearance for later card sightings.
The main referee in a neon green shirt is visible on the pitch in wide shots, e.g., at 331.1s (Cell 7).
A fourth official is visible holding a substitution board between 337.4s and 344.7s. This is a visual instance of a referee/official.
At approximately 371s (Cell 52), the referee gives a yellow card to Argentina player Enzo Fernandez (#24). This is the first yellow card found in this segment.
At approximately 375s (Cell 63), the referee gives a yellow card to France player Adrien Rabiot (#14). This is the second yellow card found in this segment.

At 376.9s (Cell 4) through 379.2s (Cell 8), an on-screen graphic confirms that player number 24, Enzo Fernández, received a yellow card. This helps the search task because it identifies the recipient of the card seen in the previous frames.
No other yellow cards are visible in the remainder of the video sequence from 376.9s to 405.5s.
The referee is also visible in the background of cells 13-15 during a goal-mouth scramble. This helps the search task by identifying another instance of the referee's presence.
The referee (wearing a red shirt) is visible in cells 24-28, standing near players during a substitution break. This helps the search task by identifying the referee's presence in the video.

The referee is visible in cells 32-36, managing a substitution. This helps the search task of scanning for visual instances of a referee.
Referee is visible again while players are preparing for the game to resume.

At 00:08:44 (game clock 54:12), the referee shows a yellow card to Adrien Rabiot (France). This is the first yellow card.
Referee is visible pointing to the penalty spot. This is a key moment where a card might be given.

No yellow card is shown by the referee in these frames or throughout the rest of the provided video segment (up to 595.4s). This helps answer the task by confirming zero yellow cards are visible in this portion of the video.

At approximately 761.6s (Cell 29) to 771.5s (Cell 55), the referee is seen holding and then showing a yellow card. Specifically, in Cell 57 (771.9s), the referee shows a yellow card to Olivier Giroud on the French bench. This is one yellow card instance.

In the montage at 1056.1s (covering timestamps from 115:46 to 116:05), no referee is visible in any of the five frames. This helps by confirming that this specific segment does not contain a visual instance of a referee or a yellow card being shown.

At 00:18:18 (game clock 113:42), the referee shows a yellow card to Leandro Paredes (Argentina). This is the fifth yellow card.

The referee, wearing a neon yellow shirt, is visible in the center of the pitch in all frames at 1151.0s. This is a direct visual instance of a referee as requested by the search task.

At 00:23:25 (during penalty shootout), the referee shows a yellow card to Emiliano Martínez (Argentina) for unsportsmanlike conduct. This is the seventh yellow card.
